## Findings

### Finding 1 — `/login` + `/register` disallowed in robots.txt (POLICY CONTRADICTION)

**Severity:** P1 launch-day

Per memory `feedback_auth_pages_indexing_policy.md`:
> entry points (`/login`, `/register`) indexable для branded search; recovery routes (`/forgot-password`, `/reset-password`, token-bound) noindex

Current robots.txt disallows `/login`, `/register`, `/forgot-password` — but the policy decision says **entry points should be indexable**. Branded queries like "lancerwise login" cannot be served if these are blocked, harming UX (users search for the brand name to find login page).

**Action для [AGENT 2]:**
1. Remove `Disallow: /login` and `Disallow: /register` from `public/robots.txt`
2. Keep `Disallow: /forgot-password` (recovery flow, no SEO value)
3. Add `/login` and `/register` to `src/app/sitemap.ts` static pages (priority 0.4, weekly)
4. Verify pages have `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">` (per-page noindex would override sitemap)

### Finding 2 — `/changelog` listed but high churn

`/changelog` has `priority: 0.6 + changeFrequency: weekly` but per memory `project_lancerwise_changelog_accordion.md`, the page is 36,969px tall on mobile (excessive). Indexing is fine but consider canonical accordion for ranking.

**Action:** Not a sitemap issue — already в backlog.

### Finding 3 — Dynamic public pages NOT in sitemap (intentional)

Per `sitemap.ts` line 31-33, token-based pages (`/profile/[handle]`, `/portfolio/[handle]`, `/card/[username]`, etc.) are intentionally omitted. These should be indexed via per-page canonicals once published by users. **No action.**

### Finding 4 — Only 1 tool exists, multiple tool URLs in copy

App has only `/tools/rate-calculator`. The repo references "Freelance Positioning Statement Generator" (commit 76d538d7), "Quick Tools Hub" (commit 6c8a1685), etc. — but these are gated behind `(app)` group (authed), not public marketing tools.

**Action:** Optional post-launch — extract specific tools into `(public-tools)` group for SEO long-tail keyword wins. Not launch-blocking.

### Finding 5 — Missing Hreflang for RU localization

Sitemap entries are single-locale (`en` implicit). The app supports RU. For dual-locale SEO, ideal sitemap pattern:

```xml
<url>
  <loc>https://www.lancerwise.com/pricing</loc>
  <xhtml:link rel="alternate" hreflang="en" href="https://www.lancerwise.com/pricing"/>
  <xhtml:link rel="alternate" hreflang="ru" href="https://www.lancerwise.com/pricing?lang=ru"/>
</url>
```

next-intl's URL strategy uses cookies/headers not URL prefix, so this would need a routing change. **P2 backlog**, not launch-blocking.

## Recommendation summary

| Finding | Priority | Owner | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1. /login + /register blocked | P1 | [AGENT 2] | unblock в robots.txt + add to sitemap |
| 2. /changelog churn | P3 | existing backlog | accordion |
| 3. Token-based dynamic pages | none | — | intentional |
| 4. More public tools | P2 post-launch | product | extract tools |
| 5. Hreflang for RU | P2 post-launch | [AGENT 2] | localized sitemap |
